globalContext: This project is an AI-driven code analysis tool for Visual Studio Code, designed to enhance developers' understanding of their codebase by providing insights into complexity, security vulnerabilities, and potential issues. The architecture consists of several key components: `ResultStore.ts` manages the storage and retrieval of analysis results, while `UnderstandingGenerator.ts` and `ProblemsReporter.ts` handle documentation generation and report creation in Markdown and JSON formats. The `AnalysisOrchestrator.ts` coordinates four scanners (`ComplexityScanner`, `StaticScanner`, `CrossFileTaintScanner`, and `AiScanner`) to perform a comprehensive analysis of the codebase. Each scanner is responsible for identifying specific types of issues, such as complexity, static errors, cross-file taints, and AI-driven insights. The results are then published through various publishers like `StatusBarManager.ts` and `DiagnosticsPublisher.ts`, which update the VS Code status bar and Problems panel respectively. Additional features include a code graph builder (`CodeGraphBuilder.ts`) to visualize relationships between code elements, and a list panel (`ListPanel.ts`) for displaying clickable lists of code locations in a webview. The project also includes rule sets for different programming languages (`pythonRules.ts`, `javascriptRules.ts`, etc.) and utilities for converting issue severities and parsing code (`LanguageParser.ts`).

methods.summary: Returns all stored analysis results, used by dashboard and status bar components.

methods.summary: Removes a file's analysis result from the store when the file is closed to prevent stale data.

methods.name: clear
methods.summary: Clears all stored analysis results, typically triggered by user actions like 'Clear All'.

methods.summary: Adds the enclosing function name to each issue based on its line number and the provided code graph.

methods.summary: Finds the last function definition before a given line in the same file, or returns '(file scope)' if no such function exists.

methods.name: toMarkdown
methods.summary: Converts an array of reported files into a human-readable markdown formatted string, grouping issues by file and function. It includes summary counts by severity and category, and provides detailed issue information with line numbers, severities, categories, messages, and suggestions.

methods.name: toJson
methods.summary: Converts an array of reported files into a machine-readable JSON string suitable for tools and language models. The JSON includes the generation timestamp, total number of issues, and details about each file's issues.

src/graph/ImpactAnalyzer.ts.summary: Analyzes code graph to determine impact of changes and find unused symbols.

symbols.name: ENTRY_POINTS
symbols.summary: A set of entry points that are invoked by VS Code or the language runtime, ensuring they are never reported as unused.

symbols.name: ImpactAnalyzer
symbols.summary: A class for analyzing the impact of changes to symbols in a code graph, providing methods to find direct callers, callees, and affected nodes.

methods.name: constructor
methods.summary: Initializes an instance of ImpactAnalyzer with a given CodeGraph.

methods.name: analyze
methods.summary: Determines the full impact of changing a symbol by its direct callers, direct callees, and all transitively affected nodes.

methods.name: blastRadiusForFile
methods.summary: Calculates the number of distinct files that would be affected if a change is made to symbols in a specific file.

methods.name: findUnusedSymbols
methods.summary: Identifies symbols that are not called by anything else, excluding framework entry points and classes/constructors, as they may still be used dynamically or exported.

methods.name: callersOf
methods.summary: Returns symbols that directly call or import the given symbol, aiding in understanding dependencies.

methods.name: calleesOf
methods.summary: Identifies symbols directly called or imported by the given symbol, useful for tracing function calls.

methods.name: affectedBy
methods.summary: Finds all symbols affected by changes to the given symbol through recursive caller relationships, essential for impact analysis.

methods.name: findNode
methods.summary: Locates a single node by its ID within the graph, returning null if not found, aiding in precise data retrieval.

methods.name: nodesByIds
methods.summary: Retrieves multiple nodes by their IDs, ensuring uniqueness and filtering out non-existent nodes.

src/config/ConfigManager.ts.summary: Reads and manages VS Code workspace settings for AI analysis configuration.

symbols.name: ConfigManager
symbols.summary: Manages configuration settings for AI analysis, including provider details and user preferences.

methods.name: cfg
methods.summary: Retrieves the VS Code workspace configuration object for 'codeSec'.

methods.name: getAiProvider
methods.summary: Returns the configured AI backend provider (e.g., ollama, openrouter). Defaults to 'ollama' if not set.

methods.name: getAiModel
methods.summary: Returns the specified AI model name. Uses the provider's default if not provided.

methods.name: getAiApiKey
methods.summary: Provides the API key for cloud-based AI providers. Returns an empty string if no key is needed (e.g., Ollama).

methods.name: getAiBaseUrl
methods.summary: Overrides the default base URL for AI requests if running on a different port. Defaults to an empty string if not set.

methods.name: isAiEnabled
methods.summary: Determines whether AI analysis should be enabled. Defaults to true, allowing users to turn it off for performance reasons.

methods.name: isStaticEnabled
methods.summary: Enables or disables the execution of built-in static regex rules during code analysis. Defaults to true.

methods.name: getComplexityThreshold
methods.summary: Sets the complexity score threshold above which functions will be flagged. Defaults to 10 if not specified.

methods.name: getDuplicateThreshold
methods.summary: Defines the minimum line length for blocks that won't be flagged as duplicates. Defaults to 6 lines.

methods.name: getLanguages
methods.summary: Lists the programming languages to be analyzed. Defaults to a set of common languages if not configured.

methods.name: shouldAnalyzeOnSave
methods.summary: Determines if code analysis should be triggered automatically when a file is saved, defaulting to true.

src/AnalysisOrchestrator.ts.summary: Coordinates four scanners to analyze code, orchestrating a two-phase process for static and AI-driven analysis.

symbols.name: AnalysisOrchestrator
symbols.summary: Coordinates the four scanners (static, complexity, duplicate, and AI) to analyze a document, publishing results immediately and merging AI findings in the background.

methods.name: constructor
methods.summary: Initializes the orchestrator with dependencies like store, config, scanners, and an onComplete callback. Sets up maps for timers and running AI tasks.

methods.name: analyze
methods.summary: Analyzes a given text document by checking if it's supported and then either debouncing or running the analysis phases immediately.

methods.name: runPhased
methods.summary: Executes two phases of analysis: static rules (immediate) and AI (background). Merges AI results into static ones before publishing.

methods.name: runAiPhase
methods.summary: Runs the AI scanner on a document, filters out duplicate issues, merges them with existing static results, and saves/updates the result if the file is still open.

methods.name: removeCrossRulesetDuplicates
methods.summary: Removes duplicate issues across different rule sets, keeping the most specific one.

methods.name: filterAiDuplicates
methods.summary: Filters out AI-generated issues that are already covered by static rules, using a line window and concept matching.

methods.name: sameRootConcept
methods.summary: Determines if two messages describe the same security or quality concept based on predefined groups of synonyms.

methods.name: keyWords
methods.summary: Extracts meaningful keywords from an issue message, stripping filler words to improve comparison.

methods.name: deduplicate
methods.summary: Removes exact duplicate issues on the same line, ensuring no redundant alerts are shown.

methods.name: debounced
methods.summary: Debounces analysis requests for a document, running them after a delay to avoid excessive processing.
